note: Intake complete. Fresh branch release/v1.31.0 off main (v1.30.0 merge). Full autonomous pipeline authorized through release v1.31.0 (main session handles git); Dean present but hands-off. His real ~40-subscription on-device pass is the post-release arbiter. Discovery inbox written for the product-manager. Export the fnm Node PATH (Node 22.23.1 at ~/.local/share/fnm) before any npm/node/git-hook command; Node 22 + 24 dual-verify at release.

prime_hypothesis.statement: v1.29's --sleep-requests 1 was added to the LIST pass whose 5-minute ABSOLUTE timeout was NOT adjusted. A 25-video listing (multiple HTTP requests/video, +1s each, worse under throttling) can exceed 5 min -> every channel's list pass times out uniformly -> the exact production signature (identical 'yt-dlp timed out and was killed' across ~20 channels).
prime_hypothesis.evidence: Dean's production screenshots: ~20 subscription failures in one run, ALL identically 'error: yt-dlp timed out and was killed'. Read-only concurrency/timeout sweep 2026-07-12 with file:line map.
prime_hypothesis.must_verify_first: T0/P0 must verify H0's arithmetic (request budget per list pass vs the 5-min timeout at run.js:105) against the code BEFORE any fix lands. If confirmed, fix the list-pass budget mismatch (reduce/remove list-pass sleeps, raise/scale the list timeout with maxVideos, or both -- Design decides).

pillars.P0: Timeout/pacing reconciliation + diagnosable reasons. Verify H0; fix list-pass budget mismatch. Every timeout reason names phase + duration; bare 'timed out and was killed' eliminated from the codebase.
pillars.P1: Per-channel queue jobs + priority (one-shot > repull > scheduled poll). Decompose the monolithic poll gate job into one job per channel. One-shot waits <=1 channel; a hung channel blocks only itself. LOAD-BEARING invariants preserved: gate never-wedge tail property + --download-archive single-writer + strict serial spawns.
pillars.P2: Circuit breaker + inter-channel backoff. Configurable inter-channel sleep; after N consecutive channel failures (config, default ~3-5) abort remainder with honest persisted status ('run paused after N consecutive failures; retrying at <time>') + schedule backoff retry. Successes reset counter. Aborted-not-run channels keep their cutoff.
pillars.P3: Stall watchdog. Progress-aware idle timer alongside absolute ceiling: no stdout progress for N min (config, default ~10) -> SIGKILL + specific reason + retry per policy. Download pass only; list pass gets the P0-fixed budget.
pillars.P4: Durable queue. Persist accepted-but-not-started jobs (one-shots at minimum) so restart requeues instead of silently losing; every accepted job eventually produces a runlog/history line (downloaded/failed/requeued/dropped-with-reason).
pillars.P5: Queue visibility. Status/chip/history show queue position + what's ahead ('queued -- 3 ahead'); repull busy-coalescing surfaced (v1.29 body fields exist -> render everywhere relevant); one-shot activity entries reflect queued->running transitions with the wait visible.
pillars.P6: yt-dlp version surface (small). Expose the probed binary version in the UI with a staleness note; document the pin-bump update path. NO runtime auto-update (locked decision D5).

out_of_scope: Parallel yt-dlp processes (stays strictly serial -- archive-file invariant + YouTube-friendliness both demand it).
Cookie acquisition automation (mounting cookies is Dean's env-side lever; existing configured-but-missing warning stays).
Any UI redesign beyond the P5 visibility additions.

constraints.v1.29_regression_surface: Outcome classification (success/partial/error/cancelled), zero-attributed=>error, cancel-latch ordering, runlog schema (ADDITIVE evolution only), retry affordances, AC6.3 argv byte-identity posture (new flags/args additive with the same injection-guard discipline: literals or validated values only, far from '--').
constraints.v1.30_regression_surface: Chip conformance ACs, one-shot three-surface visibility, BUG-2 reload-never.
constraints.spawn_discipline: shell:false arg-array spawning; SIGKILL discipline (tech-debt #24 -- process GROUP kill remains out of scope unless P3 makes it trivially safe).
constraints.queue_persistence: Follow crash-safety posture (atomic writes; bounded file). No new runtime deps.
constraints.config: All new knobs FILETUBE_YTDLP_* env vars, bounds-checked in config.js per the established parse* pattern.
constraints.verification: node:test; ESLint clean; Node 22 + Node 24 both green at release. Two-reviewer gate before acceptance; honest scoring; Dean's on-device pass (real ~40-subscription instance) is final arbiter.
constraints.reliability_budget: Per docs/RELIABILITY.md: never let one bad file/channel take down a run; explicit status codes + JSON error bodies; child processes handle both error+close; graceful degrade over crash; tech-debt items filed to the tracker.

gate_weight_map.P1_invariants: HEAVIEST. runExclusive never-wedge tail property AND --download-archive single-writer invariant AND strict serialization of spawns -- all must be proven to survive the queue decomposition in BOTH directions (property holds AND its violation is caught).
gate_weight_map.P0_H0_arithmetic: HIGH. The diagnosis must be verified against code before the fix; the phase-named reasons need exact-string criteria.
gate_weight_map.P2_P3_P4_stateful_policy: HIGH. Breaker/watchdog/durable-queue add stateful failure policy to a crash-safety-sensitive orchestrator; require deterministic fixture-driven (no live network) criteria.
gate_weight_map.v1.29_v1.30_regressions: HIGH. AC-FM both-directions style; additive-only schema; chip/three-surface/reload-never intact.

key_files_from_sweep.monolithic_poll_gate_job: lib/ytdlp/index.js:1943-1963 (entire poll run = single gate job)
key_files_from_sweep.v1.29_pacing_flags: lib/ytdlp/args.js:581-620 (--sleep-requests etc; act only INSIDE one channel invocation)

GD-1 -- avatar glyph first-letter + deterministic color (hash-letter reverted per unanimous two-reviewer consensus; Dean may re-open on-device).
AC4.5 (carried from v1.29) -- navigate-during-download FEEL of the non-blocking one-shot; code-complete + test-verified, awaits Dean's on-device confirmation.
last_completed_feature.note: v1.30 SHIPPED (released_as v1.30.0). Retained here only for the OPEN Dean-on-device ledger carried into v1.31's on-device pass. Full v1.30 record archived in git history of feature-state.json / the completed exec-plan.

State initialized carrying H0 prime hypothesis, pillars P0-P6, constraints (v1.29+v1.30 regression surfaces, spawn/persistence/config/verification), and the gate-weight map (HEAVIEST on P1 invariants: never-wedge tail + archive single-writer + strict serial spawns). Discovery inbox written for the product-manager: requirements + observable ACs for the 7 deliverables with deterministic fixture-driven (no live network) criteria for breaker/watchdog/queue, exact-string criteria for phase-named timeout reasons, and both-directions criteria for the P1 invariants in the v1.29 AC-FM style.
